blocks.content: Nathan reached into his jacket pocket. He produced the ring with the careful ceremony of a man who has not lost it, which Ethan appreciated.

blocks.speaker: Pastor
blocks.content: These rings are a symbol of the vows you have just made — a circle with no beginning and no end, a promise made visible. May they remind you, on every ordinary day and every extraordinary one, of what you have chosen here.

He held her left hand — the hand with the engagement ring she'd been wearing for two months, the warm-stoned ring he'd chosen in an estate jewelry shop on a Tuesday afternoon because it looked like the afternoon light through arcade windows. He moved it aside. He held the wedding band over her finger.

blocks.speaker: Pastor
blocks.content: Ethan, repeat after me. With this ring, I thee wed.

blocks.speaker: Ethan
blocks.content: With this ring, I thee wed.
He slid it onto her finger.
He looked at her hand. The band there, catching the candlelight. Simple and permanent and entirely right.
